item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the setup, VHS tapes are a soft spot of mine, but you don't really do anything with it. The test logs don't really go anywhere. The last addenda and note don't really add anything we didn't already know. I think going further with the media alterations and coming up with something more meaningful for the delivery guy would make this a much better article. An anomalous shill demigod from the dimension of the 80's keeping up tape deliveries across parallel dimensions, perhaps?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This mostly just sits there - it's an anomalous item but doesn't cohere into a story or provide any other interest. That and the slightly choppy writing earned a downvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll fix that up. I let my love of the word "fortnight" get the better of me, it seems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does seem out of place. Weird note to end on

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the use of "a fortnight" proper in SCP documentation? Seems a bit unscientific.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding this statement with a few changes. For me, the problem with the article is that it fizzles out after the anomaly is explained and doesn't leave that much of an impression. Also, I would recommend a prize more interesting than "free merchandise" if possible. Also, a large chunk of the article is spent focused on the deliveryman when they don't play that big of a role in the story, whatever that story may be.

Overall: good concept, but needs some polishing. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This starts off as something that could've turned out cool with the 'spot the difference', but the outcome is boring, and the focus on -1 doesn't ever manifest into anything noteworthy. I'd take this one back to the drawing board.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
My bad! The image is from the following link:

https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/5/54/UPS_truck_-804051.jpg

To my understanding, wikimedia commons is free to use. If I am wrong, you are free to remove the image from my article. I'll be sure to place the source in this thread as well. Pardon me for not doing so earlier.

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page. If the images are not wholly your own, please post the image's source as a link (or links) in reply to this post and edit it into your first post in this discussion thread.

Edit: File is public domain. File source here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:UPS_truck_-804051.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much! I'm so happy that this may actually end up succeeding.

Edit: Image source is https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/5/54/UPS_truck_-804051.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1
This is actually how the Foundation is funded. Those fronts, the soap stores and laundromats, they're not even owned by us. We just sell memorabilia online.

Getting back to scp itself, it's really well written. I constantly felt the need to keep reading this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like these SCPs that have anomalies based on weird aspects of popular culture that most people normally don't think about. I'd be interested in learning a bit more about the origins of the object, though, or what happens when more unusual tapes are used.
